description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring green, brown, and gray tones with roundel insignia and tail markings reminiscent of World War II British aircraft. The airplane has a high-wing configuration, with straight wings extending broadly from the fuselage and a single vertical stabilizer at the tail. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a tailwheel, typical of a taildragger configuration. The engine is a single piston type, mounted at the nose, with a two-blade propeller. The surface finish is matte, and the structure is primarily metal, with some glass for the cockpit and rubber for the tires.
description: A camouflage-painted, single-propeller military airplane with high straight wings, fixed taildragger landing gear, and a forward enclosed cockpit. Markings and design evoke a classic WWII British aircraft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the high straight wings, nose-mounted propeller, and fixed main landing gear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the camouflage pattern, roundel insignia, and tail markings, as well as the cockpit placement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail structure, single vertical stabilizer, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with visible propeller, cockpit, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
